change: Refactor _run_once() rate-limit retry logic (lines ~430-490) into a dedicated _retry_with_backoff() helper function. Currently the rate-limit detection, backoff sleep, and re-spawn logic is inlined in _run_once(), adding ~60 lines of complexity to an already 100-line function. Extract it into a helper that takes (prompt, env, provider, attempt) and returns the same (success, output_lines, tool_calls, final_text, proc, stderr) tuple. This reduces _run_once to ~50 lines and makes rate-limit behavior independently testable.
reason: agent.py is the weakest module at 504 lines with _run_once containing duplicated rate-limit retry logic. Extracting a helper reduces complexity and enables targeted testing of backoff behavior.

now: Refactor _run_once() rate-limit retry into _retry_with_backoff() helper in agent.py
next: Add structured failure taxonomy with per-class fitness weights beyond auth/unknown
Compress old fitness_history entries — add rolling window decay
Improve prompt_quality benchmark signal — currently static pre-auth
later: Cross-session learning transfer via structured memory compression
Adaptive cooldown based on historical rate-limit time-series patterns

diagnosis.weakest_module: agent.py — 504 lines, _run_once has 100+ lines with inlined rate-limit retry
diagnosis.fix_applied: Infra failures (auth/rate_limit) now get 0.0 fitness and skip bandit updates in evolution_engine.record_cycle()
diagnosis.improvement_impact: Prevents auth outages from dragging fitness EMA down and poisoning bandit arm selection
